description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file to resolve {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} template placeholders in Part 5 HTML. data/kill-shots.json already created (6 entries). EXP-060 replacement_html uses these placeholders but they are currently unresolved. Current Part 5 uses EXP-057 prose (hardcoded). When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML to get data-driven count injection.
issues.notes: Not urgent — existing Part 5 HTML is complete and correct via EXP-057. This is a refactoring to data-driven counts. Lower priority than content work.

issues.description: External report #1 (GitHub): Positive feedback from reader (Devilwench). No scientific error claimed. No action required.
issues.wontfix_reason: Not a problem report — reader expressing support. No kernel of truth or correction to evaluate.

issues.description: Part 4 and Part 5 each contain one inline italic transition paragraph using duplicated styling (font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-bottom:0.8em). Curmudgeon flagged in part4-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json hole #5 and part5-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json hole #4 as out-of-scope for those EXPs but recommended consolidation. Recommended fix: migrate both transitions to a shared CSS class (e.g., .section-transition) defined in the stylesheet to ease global theme changes. Status check confirms styles still inline as of 2026-04-14.
issues.notes: Filed during human-requested audit of old reports for deferred-but-untracked work. The two curmudgeon reviews flagged this as out-of-scope for their respective EXPs but never resulted in a tracked work item.

issues.description: EXP-052 is missing from expansion-tracker.json (gap between EXP-051 and EXP-053). Persists from prior integrity reports. Likely a lost ID from a write collision or abandoned expansion. No content is missing — all surrounding expansions are complete.
issues.wontfix_reason: Gap is benign — no content lost. EXP IDs are not referenced by any build process. Logging for auditability only.
issues.notes: Filed and immediately wontfix to clear from integrity backlog.

issues.description: Dome site reuses WIN-057 for two distinct claims: "Two-Zone Disc Topology" (our coverage, Prospective Weekly) and "SAA Simultaneous Acceleration" (Prospective list, uncovered by our review). The SAA Simultaneous Acceleration claim is a separate prospective claim that our wins.json does not evaluate. The dome number collision is itself evidence of numbering instability we critique, but the content divergence is an analytical coverage gap.
issues.notes: Analyst should decide: (a) add WIN-057b for SAA Simultaneous Acceleration (mirrors 058/058b pattern), or (b) add a note in WIN-057 prose about the numbering collision. Not urgent — the SAA claim is prospective with no current test window closure.

issues.description: WIN-027 and WIN-057 list different "four fitted parameters" for V50.6 H(r). WIN-027 includes ellipse axis ratio but omits 14,105 km; WIN-057 includes 14,105 km but omits ellipse ratio. True union is at least five parameters. Pick one canonical list and use it in both WINs.

QF27/QF28 nonstop framing is fragile — Qantas has operated this corridor both nonstop and via Auckland. Underlying kill-shot (30,000 km disc route vs 11,338 km flight) holds either way; soften to "Direct Sydney-Santiago services" with reference to in-air time.

WIN-027 detail_evidence opens with circular-sounding "the correction exists because the Earth is curved, not because it is flat." Rephrase as "the correction exists because any sphere-to-plane projection produces exactly this distortion pattern — the dome rediscovered the curvature it denies."

detail_verdict_text appends the standardized post-hoc boilerplate paragraph that duplicates what detail_evidence already says with more specificity. Either drop the boilerplate or trim to one sentence with anchor-link to Part 3.1b.

WIN-068 detail_evidence P3 discusses E001-E009 2026 eclipse predictions. WIN-068 is about the historical 1991-2016 Kim & Chang ensemble, not future predictions. Move the E001-E009/FSF cascade content to Section 1.8 or predictions catalog for E001-E009.

detail_verdict_text says Kim & Chang data was available "well before" dome incorporated it, without a specific date. Specify V51.0 (early 2026) as dome-incorporation version, contrasting with 2018 paper publication — nine-year gap is stronger than "well before".

WIN-010 (BOU 2017) and WIN-025 (2024 9-station) share the same dome mechanism and verdict as WIN-068 but do NOT include refraction cascade arguments. If cascade applies to ensemble, it applies to all three equally; if not (per 2026-04-13 revert), WIN-068 cross-reference to Section 1.8 should be matched in WIN-010 and WIN-025 for consistency.

WIN-044 code_analysis.monitoring tagged none but monitor.py Domain 10 line ~515 applies hardcoded per-station FSF values at eclipse time — correct tag is hardcoded. Defer update until eclipse day (2026-08-12) when observed slot is populated; tag should update automatically post-eclipse.
